title: picea jezoensis subsp. hondoensis
intro: Picea jezoensis subsp. hondoensis, also known as Japanese black spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to Japan. The bark and needles of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Picea jezoensis subsp. hondoensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions made from its bark and needles, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
